jurisdictions.name: US - New York
jurisdictions.design_logic_detail: Legislative: same session, different strategies for different sectors
jurisdictions.key_finding: Deployed disclosure and prohibition simultaneously for different sectors, not sequentially

instruments.name: A6765/S7033 — Algorithmic Pricing Disclosure Act (Nov 2025)
instruments.summary: Requires disclosure 'THIS PRICE WAS SET BY AN ALGORITHM USING YOUR PERSONAL DATA' for personalized algorithmically-set prices
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: NY AG; civil penalties up to $1,000/violation

timeline.event: Signed into law (part of FY 2026 Budget)
First Amendment challenge dismissed (NRF v. James)
AG James sends formal inquiry to Instacart

instruments.operative_institution: NY AG (state enforcement; cure period before action)

instruments.name: S7882 — Algorithmic Rent Pricing Prohibition (Dec 2025)
instruments.summary: Prohibits algorithmic pricing tools performing coordinating function in residential rent-setting
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: NY AG (civil and criminal); private right of action under Donnelly Act with treble damages
instruments.operative_institution: NY AG (civil and criminal) + private right of action (treble damages)

instruments.name: S8623 — Algorithmic Pricing Prohibition (proposed Dec 2025)
instruments.summary: Would ban personalized algorithmic pricing broadly; referred to Rules Committee
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Would include private right of action
instruments.political_economy_detail: Referred to Rules Committee; no hearing or vote scheduled

jurisdictions.name: US - California
jurisdictions.design_logic_detail: Competition enacted, prohibition defeated, AG pivoted to privacy. Not designed as a set
jurisdictions.key_finding: Legislature permits antitrust intervention against coordination but resists direct prohibition of personalization

instruments.name: AB 325 — Common Pricing Algorithms Act (Jan 2026)
instruments.summary: Amends Cartwright Act to create liability for sharing competitively sensitive data through common pricing algorithms
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: AG (enhanced criminal/civil penalties); private action under Cartwright Act with treble damages
instruments.operative_institution: AG + private action under Cartwright Act (treble damages)

instruments.name: SB 763 — Antitrust Pleading Standard Reform (Jan 2026)
instruments.summary: Lowers antitrust pleading standard, making it easier for private plaintiffs in algorithmic pricing cases
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Private action (lowered pleading burden); AG

instruments.name: AG v. RealPage / Greystar (Jan 2025-Nov 2025)
instruments.summary: Greystar settled for $7M; RealPage litigation pending
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: AG under UCL; nine-state coalition

instruments.name: AG CCPA Surveillance Pricing Sweep (Jan 2026)
instruments.summary: First enforcement action reaching proprietary personalized pricing via CCPA purpose limitation theory
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: AG investigative authority under CCPA

timeline.event: Investigative sweep announced (Data Privacy Day, Jan 28, 2026)

instruments.name: AB 446 — Surveillance Pricing Protection Act (Feb 2025)
instruments.summary: Broad prohibition withdrawn after being gutted to grocery-only by Senate Appropriations
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Would have included private right of action

timeline.event: Senate Appropriations narrowed to grocery only

instruments.political_economy_detail: Senate Appropriations gutted to grocery-only; sponsor withdrew; industry opposition ($11.7M to legislators)

instruments.name: SB 259 — Fair Online Pricing Act (2025)
instruments.summary: Would have prohibited setting online prices based on device data and geolocation
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Would have included private right of action

instruments.name: SB 295 — Preventing Algorithmic Collusion Act (2025)
instruments.summary: Failed third reading but carries over to 2026 session
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Would have included affirmative defense for due diligence
instruments.political_economy_detail: Failed third reading; carries over to 2026 session

jurisdictions.name: US - Federal
jurisdictions.design_logic_detail: Enforcement-driven: different institutional actors (DOJ, FTC, Congress, White House) with partisan discontinuity
jurisdictions.key_finding: Only operative instruments are antitrust enforcement actions inherited from prior administration; consumer protection track stalled

instruments.name: FTC 6(b) Surveillance Pricing Study (July 2024)
instruments.summary: Found 250+ retailers use surveillance pricing; public comment withdrawn by new FTC Chair
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: FTC 6(b) compulsory orders; informational only

timeline.event: FTC issues 6(b) orders to eight intermediary companies
New FTC Chair closes public comment period
Bipartisan Senate group urges FTC to reopen investigation

instruments.operative_institution: FTC Commission (politically contingent: paused by new Chair)
instruments.political_economy_detail: Public comment closed by new FTC Chair Ferguson (Jan 2025); partisan discontinuity

instruments.name: DOJ v. RealPage (filed Aug 2024)
instruments.summary: Settlement: RealPage must stop using nonpublic competitor data. Seven-year decree
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: DOJ Antitrust Division; Sherman Act; court-appointed monitor for 3 years

instruments.name: FTC v. Amazon.com (filed Sept 2023)
instruments.summary: Project Nessie generated $1.4B in excess revenue; standalone Section 5 claim upheld; trial Feb 2027
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: FTC + 19 state AGs; Sherman Act s.2; FTC Act s.5

timeline.event: Court denies Amazon's motion to dismiss; standalone Section 5 upheld

instruments.formal_institution: Market structure (Sherman Act s.2 + FTC Act s.5)

instruments.name: Preventing Algorithmic Collusion Act, S. 232 (Jan 2025)
instruments.summary: Would codify RealPage enforcement theory; does not reach unilateral personalized pricing
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: DOJ + FTC joint enforcement; civil penalties min $10,000/day
instruments.political_economy_detail: Referred to Senate Judiciary Committee; no Republican co-sponsors; hostile environment

instruments.name: Stop AI Price Gouging and Wage Fixing Act, H.R. 4640 (July 2025)
instruments.summary: First federal proposal to ban AI-based personalized pricing; near-zero passage prospect
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: FTC + state AGs + private right of action
instruments.political_economy_detail: Progressive Democrat sponsors only; near-zero passage prospect in 119th Congress

instruments.name: EO 14110 — Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy AI (Oct 2023)
instruments.summary: Revoked by Trump administration on Jan 20, 2025
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Federal agencies under existing authority
instruments.political_economy_detail: Revoked by incoming administration; replaced by pro-innovation EO 14179

instruments.name: CEA Issue Brief — Cost of Anticompetitive Pricing Algorithms (Dec 2024)
instruments.summary: Quantified $3.8B annual cost to renters; $70/month average increase in algorithm-utilizing buildings
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: No enforcement mechanism; evidentiary basis for DOJ prosecution

instruments.name: CEA/OSTP — Big Data and Differential Pricing (Feb 2015)
instruments.summary: First federal acknowledgment of algorithmic pricing as policy concern
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: No enforcement mechanism; recommended enforcement of existing laws

instruments.name: FTC Section 5 Authority (15 U.S.C. s. 45)
instruments.summary: General-purpose enforcement authority; scope under active contestation
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: FTC administrative enforcement; consent orders; federal court injunctions
instruments.operative_institution: FTC (scope under active contestation; 2022 Policy Statement politically threatened)

jurisdictions.name: US - State Bills (Composite)
jurisdictions.design_logic_detail: Decentralized, uncoordinated: 51 bills across 24 states with no model bill
jurisdictions.key_finding: Prohibition dominates proposals but fails >90%; only disclosure (NY) and sector-specific grocery bans pass

instruments.name: CO HB 1264 — Surveillance Pricing Prohibition (2025)
instruments.summary: Broadest general prohibition in 2025. Postponed indefinitely in committee
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Would have included AG + DA + private right of action

timeline.event: Postponed indefinitely in House Judiciary Committee

instruments.political_economy_detail: Postponed indefinitely in committee despite consumer group support

instruments.name: NM HB 285 — Dynamic Pricing in Grocery (2026)
instruments.summary: Passed both chambers unanimously (67-0, 40-0); prohibits dynamic pricing in grocery based on personal data
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: AG enforcement under Unfair Practices Act
instruments.political_economy_detail: Bipartisan unanimous passage; grocery necessity framing neutralized industry opposition

instruments.name: IL HB 4248 — Algorithmic Pricing Transparency Act (2025)
instruments.summary: Disclosure + opt-out right + partial prohibition; hybrid design
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: AG enforcement

instruments.name: PA HB 1942 — Surveillance Pricing Prohibition (2025)
instruments.summary: 15 House Democrat sponsors; committee hearing November 2025
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Civil penalties up to $12,500/violation

instruments.name: WA HB 2481 — Fair Pricing and Transparency Act (2026)
instruments.summary: Combines surveillance pricing prohibition with 4-year electronic shelf label moratorium
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Consumer Protection Act; DOC study mandated
instruments.political_economy_detail: Cleared committee; combines pricing prohibition with ESL moratorium

instruments.name: MD SB 387 — Protection from Predatory Pricing Act (2026)
instruments.summary: Governor-priority bill; bars grocery stores from real-time ESL price adjustments and surveillance pricing
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: AG enforcement; civil penalties up to $10,000/$25,000

instruments.name: TX SB 2567 — AI/Algorithmic Pricing Disclosure (2025)
instruments.summary: Lightest-touch approach: disclosure at point of sale, enforced through existing consumer protection
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Deceptive trade practice under DTPA
instruments.formal_institution: Market discipline (disclosure at point of sale)

instruments.name: OH SB 79 — Pricing Algorithm Disclosure (2025)
instruments.summary: Targets nonpublic competitor data in pricing algorithms; competition-adjacent disclosure
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: AG enforcement

instruments.name: GA SB 164 — Surveillance Pricing Prohibition (2025)
instruments.summary: Replicated prohibition-plus-wage structure; did not advance from committee
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Would have included AG + private right of action

instruments.name: MN HF 2452/SF 3098 — Dynamic Pricing Prohibition (2025)
instruments.summary: Did not advance from committee
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Would have included AG enforcement

instruments.name: KY HB 33 — Kentucky Price Fairness Act (2026)
instruments.summary: Embeds surveillance pricing prohibition within existing privacy framework
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: AG enforcement under KCDPA

instruments.name: VA HB 121 — Surveillance Pricing Prohibition (2026)
instruments.summary: Introduced January 2026
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: AG enforcement

instruments.name: FL SB 1746 — Surveillance Pricing (2026)
instruments.summary: Prohibition + disclosure hybrid
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: AG enforcement

instruments.name: NJ S 3612 — Biometric/Personal Data Pricing (2025)
instruments.summary: Targets use of biometric and personal data in pricing
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Division of Consumer Affairs enforcement

instruments.name: MA HB 99 — Grocery Biometric Pricing (2025)
instruments.summary: Targets biometric data use in grocery pricing
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: AG enforcement

instruments.name: TN HB 1468/SB 1807 — Surveillance Pricing (2026)
instruments.summary: Proposed effective date July 1, 2026
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: AG enforcement

jurisdictions.name: China
jurisdictions.design_logic_detail: Unified state prohibition. Every instrument tightens the constraint in the same direction
jurisdictions.key_finding: Densest instrument set globally. Deploys all strategies but operationally trusts only the state

instruments.name: Rules for Price Behavior on Internet Platforms (Dec 2025, eff. Apr 2026)
instruments.summary: Direct prohibition on algorithmic price discrimination (Article 15); targets 'big data backstabbing'
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: NDRC, SAMR, CAC jointly; penalties per existing Price Law framework

instruments.name: PIPL Article 24 — Automated Decision-Making (Nov 2021)
instruments.summary: Prohibits 'unreasonable differential treatment' in transaction prices; right to refuse automated decisions
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: CAC primary; fines up to CNY 50M or 5% of prior year revenue
instruments.formal_institution: Individual rights (right to refuse automated decisions)

instruments.name: E-Commerce Law Article 18 (Jan 2019)
instruments.summary: Requires non-personalized search options. Earliest Chinese legislative response to dashuju shashu
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: SAMR; fines CNY 50,000-500,000
instruments.formal_institution: Market discipline (non-personalized options for consumer choice)

instruments.name: Anti-Monopoly Law (2022 revision, eff. Aug 2022)
instruments.summary: Article 9 blanket prohibition on using data/algorithms for anticompetitive conduct
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: SAMR; fines up to 10% of prior year revenue; criminal liability

instruments.name: Algorithm Recommendation Management Provisions (Mar 2022)
instruments.summary: Algorithm registry (1,400+ filed); user profile control; transparency requirements
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: CAC, SAMR, MIIT; fines up to CNY 100,000; algorithm filing requirement
instruments.operative_institution: CAC + SAMR + MIIT + Ministry of Public Security

instruments.name: Anti-Unfair Competition Law (2025 revision, eff. Oct 2025)
instruments.summary: Prohibits algorithmic differential treatment and forcing below-cost sales
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: SAMR; fines, injunctions

instruments.name: Pricing Law Revision (draft July 2025)
instruments.summary: Would elevate algorithmic pricing prohibition to general statutory framework; first overhaul in 27 years
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: NDRC, SAMR
instruments.political_economy_detail: On NPC legislative agenda; not yet enacted

jurisdictions.name: European Union
jurisdictions.design_logic_detail: Dense regulatory surface from different eras, legal bases, and policy objectives. No coordinated design
jurisdictions.key_finding: Occupies every strategy except prohibition; no single instrument was designed for surveillance pricing

instruments.name: Consumer Rights Directive Art. 6(1)(ea) (Omnibus Directive, eff. May 2022)
instruments.summary: Closest EU provision to surveillance pricing. Traders must disclose when price is personalized via automated decision-making
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: National consumer protection authorities; penalties up to 4% turnover or EUR 2M

instruments.name: GDPR Article 22 — Automated Decision-Making (May 2018)
instruments.summary: Right not to be subject to solely automated decisions; applicability to pricing contested; SCHUFA ruling expanded scope
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: National DPAs; fines up to EUR 20M or 4% of global turnover

timeline.event: CJEU SCHUFA ruling expands Art. 22 scope
CJEU Dun & Bradstreet ruling on trade secret balance

instruments.formal_institution: Individual rights (right to refuse automated decisions)
instruments.operative_institution: National DPAs (no DPA has acted on pricing)

instruments.name: TFEU Articles 101/102 + 2023 Horizontal Guidelines
instruments.summary: Multiple active investigations confirmed July 2025; requires coordination (Art. 101) or dominance (Art. 102)
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: European Commission; national competition authorities; fines up to 10% global turnover
instruments.operative_institution: European Commission DG Competition + national competition authorities

instruments.name: Digital Markets Act (eff. May 2023; full application Mar 2024)
instruments.summary: Restricts data combination and self-preferencing by gatekeepers; does not prohibit personalized pricing
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: European Commission exclusively; fines up to 10% global turnover

instruments.name: AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689, eff. Aug 2024; high-risk provisions Aug 2026)
instruments.sector: Sector-specific (credit scoring and insurance pricing only)
instruments.summary: Credit/insurance pricing classified as high-risk; general algorithmic pricing NOT classified
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: National market surveillance authorities; fines up to EUR 35M or 7% global turnover
instruments.operative_institution: National market surveillance authorities + AI Office

instruments.name: Unfair Commercial Practices Directive (2005/29/EC, amended 2019)
instruments.summary: Baseline against misleading practices; not deployed specifically against personalized pricing
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: National consumer protection authorities; CPC Network
instruments.operative_institution: National consumer protection authorities + CPC Network

instruments.name: Digital Fairness Act (consultation closed Oct 2025)
instruments.summary: May address drip pricing, dynamic pricing, personalized pricing; draft expected Q3 2026
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: TBD
instruments.formal_institution: TBD (may include disclosure or prohibition of drip pricing)

instruments.name: Platform-to-Business Regulation (eff. July 2020; repeal proposed Nov 2025)
instruments.summary: B2B transparency for ranking and parity; repeal proposed as part of Digital Omnibus simplification
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: National courts; mediation

jurisdictions.name: United Kingdom
jurisdictions.design_logic_detail: Different strategies for different sectors: prohibition for insurance, disclosure for general commerce, competition for digital markets
jurisdictions.key_finding: Only jurisdiction with sector-specific prohibition on algorithmic price discrimination (FCA insurance price walking ban)

instruments.name: DMCCA 2024 Part 4 — Consumer Provisions (eff. Apr 2025)
instruments.sector: General (B2C; drip pricing and misleading omissions)
instruments.summary: CMA can now directly enforce consumer protection without court proceedings
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: CMA direct enforcement; fines up to 10% of global turnover

timeline.event: First enforcement sweep (8 investigations, 100 advisory letters)

instruments.formal_institution: Market discipline (consumer protection: drip pricing, misleading omissions)

instruments.name: CMA Dynamic Pricing Project (Nov 2024-June 2025)
instruments.summary: Guidance on dynamic and personalized pricing practices
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Non-binding; informs future enforcement

instruments.name: CMA Consumer Protection Enforcement Sweep (Nov 2025)
instruments.summary: First exercise of new DMCCA powers; advisory letters to 100 firms
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: CMA direct enforcement under DMCCA; eight investigations opened
instruments.operative_institution: CMA (new direct enforcement powers under DMCCA)

instruments.name: FCA PS21/5 — Insurance Price Walking Ban (eff. Jan 2022)
instruments.summary: Renewal price cannot exceed equivalent new business price. Eliminates loyalty penalty
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: FCA; annual senior manager attestation; pricing reports
instruments.operative_institution: FCA (annual senior manager attestation; pricing reports)

instruments.name: UK GDPR Article 22 (retained EU law, eff. May 2018; amended by DUAA 2025)
instruments.summary: Right against automated decisions; weakened by DUAA 2025
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: ICO; fines up to GBP 17.5M or 4% of global turnover

timeline.event: DUAA 2025 receives Royal Assent; weakens Art. 22 protections

instruments.name: Data (Use and Access) Act 2025 (DUAA, Royal Assent May 2025)
instruments.summary: Loosens UK GDPR automated decision-making restrictions; diverges from EU rights-based approach
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: ICO

instruments.name: Competition Act 1998 (Chapter I and II prohibitions)
instruments.summary: Anti-competitive agreements and abuse of dominance provisions
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: CMA; fines up to 10% of worldwide turnover per year (max 3 years)

instruments.name: DMCCA 2024 Part 1 — SMS Regime (eff. Jan 2025)
instruments.summary: First designations June 2025: Google, Apple
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: CMA Digital Markets Unit; fines up to 10% global turnover

instruments.name: UK Pro-Innovation AI Regulatory Framework (2023 White Paper)
instruments.summary: Principles-based, no binding obligations; sector regulators apply existing law
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Sector regulators; no dedicated AI regulator
instruments.operative_institution: Sector regulators apply existing law; no dedicated AI regulator

jurisdictions.name: Singapore
jurisdictions.design_logic_detail: Deliberate pro-innovation, principles-based. Voluntary governance across all sectors
jurisdictions.key_finding: Elaborate voluntary AI governance infrastructure, zero binding instruments targeting pricing

instruments.name: PDPA ss. 13-20 — Consent and Notification (eff. July 2014; amended 2020)
instruments.summary: No equivalent to GDPR Art. 22; no right against automated decisions
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: PDPC; fines up to SGD 1M or 10% annual turnover

instruments.name: PDPC Advisory Guidelines on AI Systems (Mar 2024)
instruments.summary: Clarifies PDPA application to AI; no pricing-specific guidance
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: PDPC (non-binding advisory)

instruments.name: Model AI Governance Framework, 2nd ed. (Jan 2020)
instruments.summary: Singapore's signature AI governance contribution; widely cited, entirely voluntary
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: None (voluntary adoption)

instruments.name: AI Verify Toolkit (2022) with CCCS AIM Plugin (2024)
instruments.summary: Open-source testing toolkit for AI governance; AIM plugin tests for anticompetitive pricing
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: None (self-assessment)

instruments.name: CPFTA — Consumer Protection (Fair Trading) Act 2003
instruments.summary: Does not address personalized pricing; targets unfair trade practices generally
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: CCCS; court-based enforcement via injunctions

instruments.name: CCCS Guidelines on Price Transparency (eff. Nov 2020)
instruments.summary: Addresses drip pricing and misleading price comparisons; not personalized pricing
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: CCCS (informs CPFTA enforcement)

instruments.name: Competition Act 2004, s. 34 — Anti-Competitive Agreements
instruments.summary: Could reach algorithmic collusion; no enforcement on pricing to date
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: CCCS; penalties up to 10% of turnover (max 3 years)

instruments.name: Competition Act 2004, s. 47 — Abuse of Dominance
instruments.summary: Requires exclusionary harm, not merely exploitative. High bar for pricing cases
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: CCCS; same penalty structure

instruments.name: CCCS Revised Competition Guidelines (eff. Feb 2022)
instruments.summary: Notes need for monitoring of algorithmic collusion; calls for further study
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: CCCS (non-binding guidance)

instruments.name: MAS FEAT Principles (Nov 2018) and Veritas Initiative
instruments.summary: Among earliest financial sector AI governance frameworks; voluntary fairness principles
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: MAS (voluntary; supervisory expectations)

instruments.name: MAS Proposed AI Risk Management Guidelines (Nov 2025)
instruments.summary: Closest approach to binding AI governance; consultation closed Jan 2026
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: MAS (supervisory expectations; not binding rules)

instruments.name: MAS Fair Dealing Guidelines (updated May 2024)
instruments.summary: Requires fair treatment but does not specifically address algorithmic pricing
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: MAS (supervisory oversight)

jurisdictions.name: South Korea
jurisdictions.design_logic_detail: Dense but indirect. Instruments from different eras addressing different problems, none specifically targeting surveillance pricing
jurisdictions.key_finding: Only APAC jurisdiction with binding dark pattern regulation reaching pricing transparency AND automated decision-making right in data protection law

instruments.name: E-Commerce Act Art. 21-2 — Dark Patterns (eff. Feb 2025)
instruments.summary: Binding dark pattern regulation reaching pricing transparency; drip pricing grace period ended Aug 2025
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: KFTC; corrective orders and administrative fines

instruments.name: PIPA Art. 37-2 — Automated Decision-Making Rights (eff. Sept 2024)
instruments.summary: Right to refuse automated decisions and demand explanation; potentially undermined by prior-consent exception
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: PIPC; fines up to 3% of revenue (10% for aggravated violations)
instruments.formal_institution: Individual rights (right to refuse automated decisions)

instruments.name: MRFTA Art. 19 — Unfair Collaborative Acts / Cartels
instruments.summary: Could reach algorithmic collusion; no enforcement on pricing algorithms to date
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: KFTC; surcharges up to 20% of relevant turnover; criminal referral

instruments.name: MRFTA Art. 3-2 — Abuse of Market Dominance
instruments.summary: Could reach exploitative algorithmic pricing by dominant firms
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: KFTC; surcharges up to 6% of relevant turnover

instruments.name: MRFTA Art. 23/45 — Unfair Trade Practices
instruments.summary: Includes price discrimination as unfair trade practice
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: KFTC; fines up to 2% of annual turnover

instruments.name: AI Basic Act (eff. Jan 2026)
instruments.summary: AI governance framework with transparency requirements for high-impact AI
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: MSIT; fines up to KRW 30M for violations; National AI Committee

instruments.name: Online Platform Fairness Act (proposed; seven bills in 22nd Assembly)
instruments.summary: Deadlocked due to U.S. trade pressure; seven competing bills
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: KFTC (proposed); fines up to 10% total revenue
instruments.political_economy_detail: Deadlocked due to U.S. trade pressure; seven competing bills

instruments.name: KFTC Platform Regulation Roadmap (Sept 2024)
instruments.summary: Structural competition reforms pending MRFTA amendments
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: KFTC

instruments.name: FSC Guidelines on AI in Financial Services (Dec 2024)
instruments.summary: Voluntary guidelines; regulatory sandbox approach
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: FSC/FSS (supervisory oversight)

jurisdictions.name: Australia
jurisdictions.design_logic_detail: Study-to-legislation pipeline: ACCC inquiry produced recommendation, which produced exposure draft bill
jurisdictions.key_finding: Gap between analytical recognition and legislative response; enforcement intensity on pricing-adjacent conduct but no targeted instruments

instruments.name: ACL s.18 — Misleading or Deceptive Conduct (eff. Jan 2011)
instruments.summary: Workhorse consumer protection; deployed against drip pricing (Webjet $9M) and illusory discounts (Coles/Woolworths)
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: ACCC; penalties up to AUD 50M or 3x benefit or 30% turnover

instruments.name: ACL s.21 — Unconscionable Conduct
instruments.summary: Broad unconscionability standard; not yet applied to algorithmic pricing
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: ACCC; same penalty structure

instruments.name: CCA s.45 — Anti-Competitive Agreements (concerted practices added 2017)
instruments.summary: Concerted practices prohibition designed for tacit coordination; untested for algorithmic collusion
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: ACCC; criminal penalties for cartel conduct (10 years imprisonment)

instruments.name: CCA s.46 — Misuse of Market Power (effects test added 2017)
instruments.summary: Effects-based test for market power misuse; could theoretically reach dominant firm pricing
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: ACCC; civil pecuniary penalties

instruments.name: ACCC Digital Platform Services Inquiry (2020-2025)
instruments.summary: Five-year inquiry; recommended economy-wide unfair trading practices prohibition
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Non-binding; informs legislative agenda

instruments.name: Unfair Trading Practices Bill 2026 (exposure draft Feb 2026)
instruments.summary: Would prohibit conduct that 'unreasonably manipulates' consumers; could theoretically reach personalized pricing
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: ACCC; penalties up to AUD 50M or 3x benefit or 30% turnover
instruments.political_economy_detail: Exposure draft consultation closed Feb 2026; proposed eff. July 2027

instruments.name: Privacy Act 1988 — APP 1 Reform: ADM Transparency (eff. Dec 2026)
instruments.summary: Requires disclosure of automated decision-making in privacy policies; weakest ADM provision in dataset
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: OAIC; infringement notice regime

instruments.name: ASIC Insurance Pricing Enforcement (IAG, QBE, RACQ — 2023-ongoing)
instruments.summary: Targets misleading loyalty discount claims. Functionally analogous to UK price walking ban but via disclosure enforcement
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: ASIC; Federal Court penalties (IAG AUD 40M, RACQ AUD 10M)

instruments.name: AI Mandatory Guardrails (consultation Sept 2024) / National AI Plan (Dec 2025)
instruments.summary: Does not address algorithmic pricing; focused on safety, accountability, transparency
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: AI Safety Institute (established Nov 2025)

jurisdictions.name: India
jurisdictions.design_logic_detail: Reactive, enforcement-triggered. Responding to specific complaints rather than addressing structural practice
jurisdictions.key_finding: Activity is enforcement-led (CCPA Uber/Ola notices). Instruments fragmented across multiple regulators with no coordination

instruments.name: Consumer Protection Act 2019, s. 2(47) — Unfair Trade Practices
instruments.summary: General unfair trade practices prohibition; enforcement against differential pricing emerging
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: CCPA; consumer commissions; penalties up to INR 10 lakh

instruments.name: Consumer Protection (E-Commerce) Rules 2020
instruments.summary: Transparency requirements for e-commerce platforms
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: CCPA; consumer commissions

instruments.name: CCPA Dark Patterns Guidelines 2023
instruments.summary: 13 specified dark patterns; self-audit model
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: CCPA; self-audit model; penalties up to INR 10 lakh

instruments.name: CCPA Enforcement — Uber/Ola Differential Pricing (Jan 2025)
instruments.summary: Notices for differential pricing based on operating systems (iPhone vs Android fares)
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: CCPA Investigation Wing

timeline.event: CCPA issues notices to Uber and Ola

instruments.name: Competition Act 2002 (amended 2023), ss. 3 and 4
instruments.summary: Anti-competitive agreements and abuse of dominance
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: CCI; penalties up to 10% of average turnover for preceding 3 years

instruments.name: CCI Market Study on AI and Competition (Oct 2025)
instruments.summary: Warning that pricing algorithms can produce collusion without human intent
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Non-binding; self-audit framework recommended

instruments.name: Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 (DPDPA)
instruments.summary: Limited automated decision-making provisions; rules notified Nov 2025
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Data Protection Board; penalties up to INR 250 crore

instruments.name: DPDP Rules 2025, Rule 13(3) — Algorithmic Impact Assessments
instruments.summary: Algorithmic impact assessments for significant data fiduciaries; phased implementation
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Data Protection Board

instruments.name: Draft Digital Competition Bill 2024 (SSDE obligations)
instruments.summary: Ex ante competition regulation for digital markets; not yet introduced in Parliament
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: CCI (proposed); penalties up to 10% global turnover
instruments.political_economy_detail: Public consultation complete; not introduced in Parliament

instruments.name: IRDAI Insurance Products Regulations 2024
instruments.summary: Product governance and pricing principles for insurance
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: IRDAI

instruments.name: RBI Digital Lending Directions 2025
instruments.summary: Transparency for digital lending; neutrality obligations for multi-lender apps
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: RBI; Key Fact Statement requirements

instruments.name: India AI Governance Guidelines (Nov 2025)
instruments.summary: Non-binding AI governance principles
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: None (voluntary)

jurisdictions.name: Indonesia
jurisdictions.design_logic_detail: Institutional fragmentation and legislative lag. Patchwork of general-purpose laws with overlapping mandates
jurisdictions.key_finding: Ride-hailing tariff cap regime (direct action) and competition law amendment with algorithmic collusion provisions in 2026 priority legislation

instruments.name: Consumer Protection Law (UU 8/1999), Arts. 9-17
instruments.summary: Unfair trade practices; enforcement limited for digital practices
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: BPSK; criminal max 2 years or IDR 500M fine

instruments.name: PDP Law (UU 27/2022), Art. 10 — Right to Object to ADM
instruments.summary: Right to object to automated decision-making; full enforcement from Oct 2024
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Komdigi; sanctions up to 2% annual revenue; criminal up to 6 years
instruments.formal_institution: Individual rights (right to object to ADM)

instruments.name: Competition Law (UU 5/1999), Arts. 5, 6, 19(d)
instruments.summary: Price fixing (Art. 5), price discrimination (Art. 6 per se illegal), discriminatory practices (Art. 19d)
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: KPPU; fines up to IDR 25B; criminal max 5 months for price discrimination
instruments.formal_institution: Market structure (antitrust: price discrimination per se illegal)

instruments.name: GR 80/2019 on E-Commerce (amended by GR 31/2023)
instruments.summary: Transparency and fair dealing principles for e-commerce
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Ministry of Trade; license revocation

instruments.name: MOT Regulation 31/2023 (Permendag 31/2023) — Anti-Price Manipulation
instruments.summary: Platform reporting obligations for suspected price manipulation
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Ministry of Trade; platform reporting to KPPU within 3 days

instruments.name: Permenhub 12/2019 — Ride-Hailing Tariff Caps
instruments.summary: Government-set tariff bands constraining algorithmic pricing; updated via Permenhub 564/2022
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Ministry of Transportation; administrative sanctions

instruments.name: OJK AI Ethics Guidelines for Fintech (Dec 2023; updated Dec 2024)
instruments.summary: Voluntary code of ethics for AI in fintech
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: OJK (supervisory expectations)

instruments.name: OJK AI Governance for Banking (Apr 2025)
instruments.summary: Voluntary AI governance guidelines for banking sector
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: OJK (supervisory guidance)

instruments.name: Draft Competition Law Amendment (2026 Priority Legislation)
instruments.summary: Algorithmic collusion provisions; Parliamentary Working Committee deliberation Feb 2026
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: KPPU (proposed expanded powers: search and seizure, digital evidence)
instruments.political_economy_detail: In 2026 priority legislation; Parliamentary deliberation ongoing

instruments.name: Draft Presidential Regulation on AI Roadmap and Ethics
instruments.summary: AI governance framework; pushed to early 2026
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Komdigi (cross-sector)

instruments.name: Draft Consumer Protection Law Overhaul
instruments.summary: Proposed institutional reforms for digital-era consumer protection
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Proposed National Consumer Protection Agency

jurisdictions.name: Germany
jurisdictions.design_logic_detail: Competition-dominated. Layers national instruments on top of EU baseline, Bundeskartellamt as both intellectual leader and enforcer
jurisdictions.key_finding: First enforcement order and monetary disgorgement against algorithmic pricing mechanisms in the EU (Amazon Section 19a)

instruments.name: GWB Section 19a — Paramount Cross-Market Significance (eff. Jan 2021)
instruments.sector: Digital markets (designated: Meta, Google, Amazon, Apple)
instruments.summary: Amazon enforcement order Feb 2026. First digital competition instrument of its kind
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Bundeskartellamt; prohibition orders; disgorgement; fines up to 10% global turnover

timeline.event: Amazon enforcement order (first algorithmic pricing disgorgement in EU)

instruments.name: GWB Sections 19/20 — Abuse of Dominance / Relative Market Power
instruments.summary: Broader than EU Art. 102: covers relative market power, not just dominance
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Bundeskartellamt; fines up to 10% global turnover
instruments.formal_institution: Market structure (abuse of dominance + relative market power)

instruments.name: Section 312d BGB / Article 246a EGBGB (Omnibus Directive, eff. May 2022)
instruments.summary: German transposition of EU personalized pricing disclosure requirement
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: State consumer protection authorities; fines up to EUR 25,000

timeline.event: German Omnibus Directive transposition took effect

instruments.name: UWG Sections 5a/5b — Misleading Omissions and Ranking Transparency
instruments.summary: Amended to implement Omnibus Directive. Competitor enforcement mechanism (Abmahnungen) is unusual
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: State authorities; competitor actions (Abmahnungen); injunctions and damages

instruments.name: PAngV — Price Indication Regulation (amended May 2022)
instruments.summary: Requires prior-price disclosure for promotions; 30-day lowest-price rule
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: State authorities; fines up to EUR 25,000

instruments.name: BDSG Section 37 — Automated Individual Decision-Making
instruments.summary: Supplements GDPR Art. 22; contested after SCHUFA ruling
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: BfDI; state DPAs; fines per GDPR schedule

instruments.name: BDSG Section 31 — Scoring in Commercial Transactions
instruments.summary: CJEU compatibility questioned; proposed deletion
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: BfDI; state DPAs

instruments.name: KI-MIG — AI Market Surveillance Act (Cabinet approved 2025)
instruments.summary: German implementation of EU AI Act; not yet enacted as of Feb 2026
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Federal Network Agency (BNetzA) as central authority
instruments.political_economy_detail: Cabinet approved; not yet enacted as of Feb 2026

instruments.name: Datenethikkommission Report (Oct 2019)
instruments.summary: Five-tier risk framework for algorithmic systems; influenced EU AI Act design
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: N/A (recommendations only)

jurisdictions.name: Canada
jurisdictions.design_logic_detail: Active motion. Substantially amended Competition Act (2022, 2024) but Bill C-27 death left no federal ADM framework
jurisdictions.key_finding: Competition Bureau's June 2025 algorithmic pricing discussion paper is the closest institutional engagement with surveillance pricing

instruments.name: Competition Act, s. 74.01(1.1) — Drip Pricing Prohibition (2022, strengthened 2024)
instruments.summary: Drip pricing prohibition, among strongest globally
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Competition Bureau; AMPs up to CAD $10M or 3% global turnover; private right of action (June 2025)

timeline.event: DoorDash action filed; private right of action takes effect

instruments.name: Competition Act, s. 45 — Criminal Conspiracy (Price-Fixing)
instruments.summary: Untested for algorithmic collusion
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Competition Bureau; imprisonment up to 14 years; fines up to CAD $25M

instruments.name: Competition Act, s. 90.1 — Civil Competitor Collaboration (amended 2024)
instruments.summary: Expanded to non-competitors if significant purpose is lessening competition
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Competition Bureau; Competition Tribunal

instruments.name: Competition Act, ss. 78-79 — Abuse of Dominance (amended 2024)
instruments.summary: Excessive pricing now an enumerated anti-competitive act
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Competition Bureau; AMPs up to 3% global turnover

instruments.name: Competition Act, s. 74.01(1) — Deceptive Marketing
instruments.summary: False/misleading representations; private right of action added June 2025
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Competition Bureau; AMPs up to CAD $10M/$15M or 3% turnover; private right of action (June 2025)

timeline.event: Private right of action takes effect (June 2025)

instruments.name: PIPEDA — Consent and Purpose Limitation
instruments.summary: General data protection; lacks automated decision-making provisions
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: OPC; findings and recommendations (non-binding); Federal Court enforcement

instruments.name: Quebec Law 25 — Automated Decision-Making (s. 12.1, eff. Sept 2023)
instruments.summary: Only Canadian jurisdiction with automated decision-making rights; directly relevant to pricing
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: CAI; AMPs up to CAD $25M or 4% global turnover
instruments.formal_institution: Individual rights (right to refuse automated decisions)
instruments.operative_institution: CAI (Commission d'acces a l'information du Quebec)

instruments.name: Treasury Board Directive on ADM (2019, amended 2023)
instruments.summary: Government-only. Early public-sector ADM governance
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: Treasury Board Secretariat; Algorithmic Impact Assessment mandatory

instruments.name: Competition Bureau — Algorithmic Pricing Discussion Paper (June 2025)
instruments.summary: 'What We Heard' report Jan 2026. Closest institutional engagement with surveillance pricing
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: N/A: policy development stage

jurisdictions.name: Netherlands
jurisdictions.design_logic_detail: Institutionally coordinated. Instruments from different eras but regulators actively talk to each other about algorithms
jurisdictions.key_finding: First EU national market investigation specifically into computer-controlled consumer pricing (ACM aviation sector); AFM found ~50% of non-life insurers charge loyal customers higher margins

instruments.name: Article 6:230m(1)(ea) BW — Personalized Pricing Disclosure (eff. May 2022)
instruments.summary: Dutch transposition of EU Omnibus Directive personalized pricing disclosure
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: ACM; fines up to EUR 900,000 or 1% of turnover

instruments.name: Section 6:193a-j BW — Unfair Commercial Practices
instruments.summary: UCPD implementation; baseline against misleading practices
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: ACM; fines; private actions for damages

timeline.event: EU Unfair Commercial Practices Directive transposed

instruments.name: ACM Leidraad Bescherming Online Consument (2020, updated May 2024)
instruments.summary: Online consumer protection guidelines
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: ACM (guidance)

instruments.name: ACM Leidraad Prijsweergave (Sept 2025) — Price Display Guidelines
instruments.summary: Price display and comparison guidelines
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: ACM; fines up to EUR 900,000 or 1% of turnover

instruments.name: Besluit Prijsaanduiding Producten — 30-Day Prior Price Rule (eff. Jan 2023)
instruments.summary: 30-day lowest-price rule for promotions
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: ACM; fines up to EUR 900,000 or 1% of turnover

instruments.name: Mededingingswet — Dutch Competition Act (Articles 6, 24)
instruments.summary: Anti-competitive agreements and abuse of dominance
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: ACM; fines up to 10% of global turnover; private damages

instruments.name: ACM Market Investigation — Computer-Controlled Prices in Aviation (2025)
instruments.summary: First EU national investigation specifically into algorithmic pricing; draft report expected early 2026
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: ACM (non-binding recommendations; may trigger enforcement)

instruments.name: UAVG — GDPR Implementation Act
instruments.summary: GDPR implementation; includes Article 22 automated decision-making provisions
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: AP (Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens); fines per GDPR schedule

instruments.name: AFM Exploratory Study on Personalized Pricing in Insurance (June 2021)
instruments.summary: First EU financial supervisor study specifically on personalized insurance pricing
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: AFM (underlying Wft obligations)

instruments.name: AFM Deep Dive: Margin Personalisation (Apr 2025)
instruments.summary: Found ~50% of non-life insurers charge loyal customers higher margins; call to action
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: AFM (underlying Wft obligations; product governance)

instruments.name: DNB Sector Update: AI at Insurers (Mar 2025)
instruments.summary: Supervisory expectations for AI use in insurance
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: DNB (prudential supervision)

instruments.name: AP as Coordinating AI Supervisor / DCA (est. 2023)
instruments.summary: Institutional coordination for algorithmic oversight; connects multiple regulators
instruments.enforcement_mechanism: AP; coordination with ACM, AFM, RDI, CvdM
